result.summary: Sentry exposes a REST API at `https://sentry.io/api/0/` and an MCP server at `https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp`; both are authenticated with OAuth 2.0 per published metadata, while the docs also advertise a `sentry-cli` CLI but its auth flow is not documented in this corpus.

sentry_oauth_app.label: Sentry OAuth 2.0 app/session
sentry_oauth_app.setup: Use Sentry's OAuth 2.0 flow against [`https://sentry.io`](https://sentry.io): authorize at [`https://sentry.io/oauth/authorize/`](https://sentry.io/oauth/authorize/) and exchange the code at [`https://sentry.io/oauth/token/`](https://sentry.io/oauth/token/). Request only the scopes you need, such as `org:read`, `project:read`, or `event:read`. Send the resulting access token as `Authorization: Bearer …`.

sentry_mcp_oauth.label: Sentry MCP OAuth 2.0 session
sentry_mcp_oauth.setup: Connect an MCP client to [`https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp`](https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp). The server advertises OAuth 2.0 with authorization server [`https://mcp.sentry.dev`](https://mcp.sentry.dev) and supports dynamic client registration, so the MCP client can onboard and complete login with that server automatically.

surfaces.name: Sentry REST API
surfaces.notes: The protected resource metadata at `https://sentry.dev/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource` identifies `https://sentry.io` as the resource, `https://sentry.io` as the authorization server, and bearer tokens in the header as the supported method.

surfaces.name: Sentry MCP server
surfaces.notes: Detected as an MCP endpoint with OAuth 2.0 authentication and dynamic client registration support. The server card at `https://sentry.dev/.well-known/mcp/server-card.json` also points to this endpoint.

surfaces.name: Sentry CLI
surfaces.notes: The docs homepage advertises a `sentry-cli` command-line interface, but this corpus does not include installation or login/authentication details.
